body:
mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale
rationale: status: open
reason: Bash tool unusable in this execution environment — host filesystem backing /tmp is full (ENOSPC). Every Bash invocation, including a bare `true`, fails with:
  ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/...'
Confirmed via Write tool as well: writing a scratch file to /tmp fails with the same ENOSPC. This blocks `go build` (needed to produce the ddx binary the test execs), `go test ./cmd/...` (needed to verify the AC), and `git add`/`git commit` (needed to land any fix). No code change was possible or verifiable without a working shell.

This is a host-level disk-space condition on the execution machine, not a defect in cli/cmd/work_provider_orphan_linux_test.go, project_runtime_preflight.go, or doctor.go. Retrying on a worker with free /tmp space should succeed.
